summary: Initial tailored resume for Zed AI Rust Engineer
Added SonicGen as project #3 (hand-written audio dedup engine, no agents) — counters systems-programming gap for Zed
Simplified project headers (just name + year), reframed Staffclaw to lead with multi-source API synthesis

Rewrote Staffclaw with full narrative: OpenClaw 92-endpoint discovery → reverse-engineered entire app (manager side too) → drop-in replacement via Teamworxs credential proxy → external APIs feed an auto-scheduler
Rewrote summary to be about Mason (capabilities + what he wants), not a project description
Removed em-dashes from prose throughout, rewrote summary to be about Mason not projects, added voice rule #14 banning em-dashes in prose
Stripped hyphenated compound adjectives from prose (cost-vs-quality, full-stack, end-to-end, human-in-the-loop, etc.); updated voice rule #14 to cover them
Replaced tilde approximations (~200, ~15,600) with 'about/around/roughly'; extended voice rule #14 to ban tildes in prose
Replaced arrow pipelines with prose descriptions; extended voice rule #14 to ban arrows in prose
Rounded LOC counts to thousands to match 'about/around' approximators (15,600 -> 15,000, 30,700 -> 30,000); added voice rule on precision matching
Dropped '6-month intensive' from bootcamp line, just the dates now; added 'intensive' to banned words
Removed 'capstone' from resume + base template (replaced with 'final project' or 'sprint at the end of the bootcamp'); added to banned word list
Refreshed gap report to match final resume (added SonicGen + Staffclaw defensibility, aligned phrasing with post voice pass resume)
Initial draft for Zed AI Rust Engineer — leads with VTR + Sobo craftsmanship essay, SonicGen as hand-written counter-evidence, closes on AI Foundations team / DeltaDB + ACP
